title: Serology Protocol for Antigen Detection
author: Dr. John Smith
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bewejfbe

steps.title: If the concentration of antigen in...
steps.instruction: If the concentration of antigen in the sample potentially exceeds the highest point of the standard curve (i.e. > 1,000 pg/ml) prepare one or more dilutions of the sample using the standard diluent.

steps.title: Typically a standard curve may span...
steps.instruction: Typically a standard curve may span concentrations from 0 to 1000 pg/ml but may go as high as 3000 pg/ml depending on the predicted amount of antigen in the sample and the amount of standard protein available. Typically two-fold or three-fold dilutions of the standard protein are prepared from the stock solution using the standard diluent. When preparing serial dilutions of a protein standard, use fresh tips after each dilution.

steps.title: Dilute the capture antibody to the...
steps.instruction: Dilute the capture antibody to the appropriate concentration allowing sufficient volume for 50-100 ul per well.

steps.title: Add the diluted capture antibody to...
steps.instruction: Add the diluted capture antibody to the plate, cover and incubate for 2 hours at room temperature (RT).

steps.title: Remove the solution and wash the...
steps.instruction: Remove the solution and wash the plate with 200 ul per well wash buffer for 3 x 5 minutes on a shaking platform.

steps.title: Add 300 ul blocking buffer per...
steps.instruction: Add 300 ul blocking buffer per well, cover the plate and incubate for 1 hour at room temperature. Alternatively block overnight at 4uC.

steps.title: Prepare the samples and standards
steps.instruction: Prepare the samples and standards. The volume per well should be the same as the capture antibody used in step 1.

steps.title: Remove the blocking buffer and add the samples and standards
steps.instruction: Remove the blocking buffer and add the samples and standards. Cover the plate and incubate for 1 hour at RT.

steps.title: Remove the solution and wash the...
steps.instruction: Remove the solution and wash the plate with 200 l per well wash buffer for 3 x 5 minutes on a haking platform.

steps.title: Dilute the biotinylated detection antibody to the appropriate concentration
steps.instruction: Dilute the biotinylated detection antibody to the appropriate concentration. The volume per well should be the same as the capture antibody used in step 1.

steps.title: Add the diluted detection antibody to...
steps.instruction: Add the diluted detection antibody to the plate, cover and incubate for 1 hour at RT.

steps.title: Remove the solution and wash the...
steps.instruction: Remove the solution and wash the plate with 200 l per well wash buffer for 3 x 5 minutes on a shaking platform.

steps.title: Dilute the enzyme conjugate to the appropriate concentration
steps.instruction: Dilute the enzyme conjugate to the appropriate concentration. The volume per well should be the ame as the capture antibody used in step 1.

steps.title: Add the diluted enzyme conjugate to...
steps.instruction: Add the diluted enzyme conjugate to the plate, cover and incubate for 1 hour at RT.

steps.title: Remove the solution and wash the...
steps.instruction: Remove the solution and wash the plate with 200 l per well wash buffer for 6 x 5 minutes on a shaking platform

steps.title: Add substrate solution to the plate
steps.instruction: Add substrate solution to the plate. The volume per well should be the same as the capture antibody used in step 1.

steps.title: Incubate the plate at RT until...
steps.instruction: Incubate the plate at RT until the desired color intensity is reached. Ideally a clear gradient will result for the standards.

steps.title: Stop the reaction by adding an...
steps.instruction: Stop the reaction by adding an equal amount of stop solution.

steps.title: If using TMB, measure the absorbance at 450 nm
steps.instruction: If using TMB, measure the absorbance at 450 nm. For other substrates use the appropriate detection technique.

steps.title: Important
steps.instruction: Important: Do not allow the plate to dry at any point
